growth_records.cite: Ehsaan et al. Chromosomal integration of the pSOL1 megaplasmid of Clostridium acetobutylicum for continuous and stable advanced biofuels production. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01714-w. [PMC11222136]
growth_records.snippet: The best compromise between residual glucose, titre and productivity was observed at a dilution rate of 0.075 h-1 with a biofuel titre of 13.4 g l-1, a productivity of 1 g l-1 h-1 and a yield of 0.31 g g-1.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.075 h^-1. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.075 = 9.24 h (calculated). Reported: biofuel(IBE) titre 13.4 g/L, volumetric productivity 1 g/L/h, mass yield 0.31 g biofuel/g glucose. Volumetric glucose consumption = productivity/yield = 1/0.31 = 3.23 g/L/h (calculated). Specific qS/qP (mmol/gDW/h) not derivable because steady-state DCW (gDW/L) is only shown graphically (Fig 2b), not stated numerically. Medium GMM per litre as defined in Methods (bioreactor used base GMM = 50 g/L glucose; the 60 g/L-glucose / ammonium-acetate modification applied only to serum-bottle batch cultures). pH 4.4, 35 C stated.

growth_records.snippet: it was possible to produce biofuels at high yield (0.3 g g-1) and productivity (1.3 g l-1 h-1) for the highest dilution rate evaluated, although under this condition high residual glucose concentrations were observed.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D. Highest dilution rate evaluated = 0.75 h^-1 (Methods: multi-stage chemostat stage 4 raised D to 0.75 h^-1; Fig 2b shows four dilution rates = 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.75). mu = 0.75 h^-1, doubling = ln2/0.75 = 0.924 h (calculated). Reported yield 0.3 g biofuel/g glucose, volumetric productivity 1.3 g/L/h. Volumetric glucose consumption = 1.3/0.3 = 4.33 g/L/h (calculated); high residual glucose noted (incomplete uptake). Confidence medium because the numeric coupling of 'highest dilution rate' to exactly 0.75 h^-1 is inferred from Methods stage list; specific qS not derivable (no numeric DCW).

growth_records.snippet: In GMM, the performances obtained in terms of yield (0.31 g g-1), titre (15.4 g l-1) and productivities (15.5 g l-1 h-1) were the highest ever reported for the continuous production of an advanced IBE fuel mixture ... a cell bleeding rate w
growth_records.notes: Membrane cell-recycle (perfusion) continuous culture with total cell retention and cell bleed at 0.05 h^-1; at steady state the specific growth rate equals the cell bleed rate, so mu ~= 0.05 h^-1 (NOT the permeate dilution rate, which is decoupled from growth because biomass is retained). Doubling = ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h (calculated). Reported: yield 0.31 g biofuel/g glucose, titre 15.4 g/L, volumetric productivity 15.5 g/L/h. Volumetric glucose consumption = 15.5/0.31 = 50 g/L/h (calculated). Specific qS/qP not derivable (biomass reported as DCW only graphically, OD600 reached 200). Confidence medium: mu=cell-bleed-rate is the standard perfusion steady-state relation but not stated verbatim.

growth_records.cite: Huanhuan Liu et al. Integrated intracellular metabolic profiling and pathway analysis approaches reveal complex metabolic regulation by Clostridium acetobutylicum. Microbial Cell Factories (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12934-016-0436-4. [PMC4753663]
growth_records.snippet: At these time points, the specific growth rate mu were 0.0708, 0.0053, 0.0010, and 0.0031 (h-1), and the specific butanol production rate q were 0.0041, 0.0524, 0.0724 and 0.0004 (h-1). ... The highest butanol production of 10.98 +/- 0.15 g
growth_records.notes: Single anaerobic batch ABE fermentation of C. acetobutylicum ATCC 824 in P2 medium (50 g/L glucose), 37 C, initial pH 6.8, N2:H2:CO2 sparge. growth_rate_per_h = max reported specific growth rate mu = 0.0708 h-1 (mid-log, 24 h); paper also reports mu = 0.0053, 0.0010, 0.0031 h-1 at 48/60/80 h. doubling_time_h = ln2/0.0708 = 0.6931/0.0708 = 9.79 h. Butanol specific production rate q reported by authors in unusual units of h-1 (0.0041/0.0524/0.0724/0.0004); captured max in secretion_rates as-reported (likely g butanol per g biomass per h). OD600 rose to ~3.96-4.32 during stationary phase. Acetate and butyrate reported only as qualitative time-courses (Fig 1); no numeric titers/rates or explicit substrate uptake rate (qS) or yields given in main text. Butanol 10.98 g/L is a fed/optimized (2 g/L yeast extract) perturbation, not the base condition, so not used as a base secretion value. Focus of paper is intracellular GC-MS metabolomics + PLS-DA pathway analysis, not full flux quantification; hence no MFA net fluxes.

growth_records.snippet: 13C MFA of C.acetobutylicum grown in chemostat ... reference condition, glucose limited condition and butanol stimulus ... 7 exchange fluxes. ... Glucose exchange -73.3 +/- 3.7; Acetate [12.96..13.016]; Acetone 12.5 +/- 0.06; Butanol [29.62
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote 13C-MFA record. Values from Table 2 (glucose-limited condition). All fluxes are NORMALIZED to the specific growth rate (growth set = 1.00 +/- 0.05); stated unit is g^-1 (CDW), so these are relative/normalized fluxes, NOT absolute mmol/gDW/h. Chromatographically measured exchange fluxes: glucose -73.3+/-3.7 (uptake), acetone 12.5+/-0.06, ethanol 6.13+/-0.31. 13C-MFA-derived (given as ranges; I report the midpoint): acetate 13.0 (=(12.96+13.016)/2), butanol 30.14 (=(29.62+30.67)/2, reported 30.1), butyrate 1.6 (=(0+3.23)/2), EPS 10.1 (=(10.01+10.26)/2). 12 internal 13C-MFA fluxes captured as mfa_fluxes ranges. IMPORTANT PROVENANCE: this paper re-analyses the C. acetobutylicum dataset of Wallenius et al. (2016) Bioresource Technol. 219:378-386 ('...in stressed chemostat conditions'); the experimental cultivation/measurements originate there, not in this paper. Absolute growth rate, medium composition, temperature and pH are NOT reported in this paper (would be in Wallenius 2016). Strain not stated. Two further conditions exist (reference: measured growth = 0.95; butanol-stimulated) but their numeric flux data are in Supplementary Table S2, not in the available full text, so no separate records could be built for them. No arithmetic on mu/doubling time possible (only normalized growth=1).

growth_records.snippet: After approximately 3 days (t = 65-80 h), stable cell densities (OD 5 +/- 0.5) and fermentation products (acetate ~35 mM, butyrate ~60 mM) indicated the beginning of phase II, balanced steady-state growth at pH 5.7.
growth_records.notes: Acidogenic steady state (phase II, pH 5.7). Growth rate = dilution rate at chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.075 h^-1 (reported D). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.075 = 9.24 h (calculated). Product titers reported as approximate steady-state values: acetate ~35 mM, butyrate ~60 mM (OD600 ~5+/-0.5). Derived volumetric secretion rates at steady state qV = D x C: acetate = 0.075 x 35 = 2.63 mmol/L/h; butyrate = 0.075 x 60 = 4.50 mmol/L/h (NOT specific per gDW because gDW biomass not reported, only OD600). Glucose uptake rate not derivable: glucose in surplus (4% wt/vol = 40 g/L; ~222 mM using MW 180.16), residual glucose not reported, no gDW biomass. Medium composition only partially stated here (0.5 mM KH2PO4, 4% glucose); full defined-medium recipe cited from Fiedler et al. 2008. Temperature 37 C, obligate anaerobe.

growth_records.snippet: The co-culture grew slower in the N2 condition and led to a 2-fold lower optical density than the NO3- condition ... The N2 condition was also the only condition where glucose was not completely consumed ... C. acetobutylicum produced 30% m
growth_records.notes: N2-fixation condition: slowest growth, 2-fold lower final OD600 than NO3- condition, glucose not fully consumed (residual), ~30% more CO2/glucose (relative, N2 fixation raises energy demand). up.method: chemostat steady-state mass balance; specific flux value reported only in Fig 3 metabolic flux map (all fluxes normalized to glucose flux = 100)

HPLC fermentation product; quantitative flux in Fig 3 (normalized to glucose=100)
gas; primary sink of reduced ferredoxin under acidogenesis (Fig 2A electron flux map)

mfa.note: All metabolic-flux-map values normalized to initial carbon source glucose = 100 under acidogenesis
Acidogenesis: reduced ferredoxin primarily used to produce H2; small fraction to NADH (butyrate) and NADPH (anabolism) — Fig 2A

growth_records.cite: Yoo, Minyeong et al. A Quantitative System-Scale Characterization of the Metabolism of Clostridium acetobutylicum. mBio (2015). DOI:10.1128/mBio.01808-15. [PMC4669385]
growth_records.snippet: acidogenesis (pH 6.3, 995 mM carbon from glucose) ... phosphate-limited chemostat cultures ... at a dilution rate of 0.05 ... acidogenic (producing acetate and butyrate) when grown at neutral pH on glucose
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat => mu = D = 0.05 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693147/0.05 = 13.86 h. Feed 995 mM carbon as glucose = 995/6 = 165.83 mM glucose. Phosphate 0.7 mM is the growth-limiting nutrient (0.5 mM in Janssen et al.). Temperature 37C, acidogenic pH 6.3. Culture conditions cited from Vasconcelos et al. (ref 6) and Girbal et al. (ref 9). Numeric specific uptake/secretion rates are presented only in the Fig 3 metabolic-flux map (image; values in mmol/gDCW/h but normalized so glucose flux = 100) and could not be read from the XML text; products listed qualitatively (acetate, butyrate, H2, CO2 for acidogenesis).

growth_records.cite: Yoo, Minyeong et al. A Quantitative System-Scale Characterization of the Metabolism of Clostridium acetobutylicum. mBio (2015). DOI:10.1128/mBio.01808-15. [PMC4669385]
growth_records.snippet: alcohologenesis (pH 6.3, 498 mM carbon from glucose and 498 mM carbon from glycerol) ... alcohologenic (forming butanol and ethanol but not acetone) when grown at neutral pH under conditions of high NAD(P)H availability
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat => mu = D = 0.05 h-1; td = ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h. Mixed feed: 498 mM carbon glucose = 498/6 = 83 mM glucose; 498 mM carbon glycerol = 498/3 = 166 mM glycerol (glycerol is C3). Phosphate-limited 0.7 mM, 37C, pH 6.3. Numeric specific rates only in Fig S3 flux map (sum of glucose+half glycerol normalized to 100); products qualitative (butanol, ethanol, H2, CO2; no acetone). Conditions cited from Vasconcelos et al. ref 6 / Girbal et al. ref 9.

growth_records.cite: Zhao X et al. A quantitative metabolomics study of high sodium response in Clostridium acetobutylicum ATCC 824 acetone-butanol-ethanol (ABE) fermentation. Scientific Reports (2016). DOI:10.1038/srep28307. [PMC4913259]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max = 0.128 +/- 0.0013 h-1 from Table 1 / Fig 2f). Doubling time calculated: ln2/0.128 = 0.693/0.128 = 5.42 h
growth_records.snippet: cells uptake xylose at a rate of 33.83 +/- 2.79 mmol gDW-1 h-1 in the NaCl group, which is 13.2% lower than in control group (38.95 +/- 3.82 mmol gDW-1 h-1). Control: mu 0.128, butanol 4.93, acetone 2.25, ethanol 3.05 mmol gDW-1 h-1 (Table
growth_records.notes: CONTROL group (only base 30 mM sodium from sodium acetate in CGM, no NaCl supplement). All rates from Table 1 (n=3, maxima from Figs 2 and 3). Xylose specific uptake 38.95 mmol/gDW/h (experimental). Specific productivities (secretion) directly reported. mu_max 0.128 h-1 => doubling time ln2/0.128=5.42 h. Fermentation temperature taken as 37 C (stated for inoculum growth; not separately restated for the batch bottles). ABE solvent ratio ~3:6:1 acetone:butanol:ethanol. Final titers and biomass captured in yields.

up.method: reported; specific xylose consumption rate at exponential growth (Table 1, Fig 2d); 13.2% lower than control

growth_records.cite: Zhao X et al. A quantitative metabolomics study of high sodium response in Clostridium acetobutylicum ATCC 824 acetone-butanol-ethanol (ABE) fermentation. Scientific Reports (2016). DOI:10.1038/srep28307. [PMC4913259]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max = 0.121 +/- 0.0023 h-1 from Table 1 / Fig 2f). Doubling time calculated: ln2/0.121 = 0.693/0.121 = 5.73 h
growth_records.snippet: cells uptake xylose at a rate of 33.83 +/- 2.79 mmol gDW-1 h-1 in the NaCl group. NaCl group specific productivities: butanol 5.09, acetone 2.54, ethanol 3.43 mmol gDW-1 h-1; mu 0.121 h-1 (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: HIGH-SODIUM (NaCl) group: base CGM (30 mM Na from acetate) + 10 g/L NaCl (170 mM) = ~200 mM total sodium threshold. All rates from Table 1 (n=3). Xylose uptake 33.83 mmol/gDW/h (experimental). Note solvent specific productivities were essentially unchanged vs control (even slightly higher) despite lower biomass/titers, the paper's central finding. mu_max 0.121 h-1 => doubling time ln2/0.121=5.73 h. Final titers and biomass in yields.
